NOT CONTESTED, NO ARGUMENT HELD. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: THE UNOPPOSED MOTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL ANSWER SET ONE INTERROGATORIES, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, WITHIN 15 DAYS. MS. DISCOVERY 302, MAY-16-2024 RE: PLAINTIFF'S NTC OF MTN COMPELLING THE COURT TO ENFORCE THE ORDER DATED OCT 24, 2023 TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY AND INTERROGATORIES. THE MATTER IS NOT ARGUED/CONTESTED. THE COURT CONFIRMS THAT ITS TENTATIVE RULING WAS NOT APPROPRIATELY CONTESTED AS SPECIFIED IN THE TENTATIVE RULING. THEREFORE, ORAL ARGUMENT WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED. THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED. SEE MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM TO WILLIAMCLYNN@GMAIL.COM. Ntc Of Mtn Compelling The Court To Enforce The Order Dated Oct 24, 2023 To Respond To Discovery And Interrogatories
Off Calendar May-16-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF CAMILLE COLARUSSO's Ntc Of Mtn Compelling The Court To Enforce The Order Dated Oct 24, 2023 To Respond To Discovery And Interrogatories.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Camille Colarusso, in propria persona, Plaintiff.
Neil Rosenbaum, Esq., of Solan, Park, & Robello, (415) 777-3300, for Defendant.

The matter is not argued/contested. The court confirms that its tentative ruling was not appropriately contested as specified in the tentative ruling. Therefore, oral argument will not be considered. The court's tentative ruling is adopted. Motion is denied without prejudice as the proof of service accompanying the moving papers shows that the moving papers were served upon the wrong address thereby depriving the other party of due process/the opportunity to be heard.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-22-2024 Continued To Jul-15-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Mtn Compelling The Court To Enforce The Order Dated October 24th 2023 To Respond To Discovery And Interrogatories
Off Calendar Feb-29-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Camille Colarusso, 201-669-1439, nicholasmichaels@icloud.com, Plaintiff in Pro Per
No other appearances.

Motion is not contested. Plaintiff stipulate to Judge Pro Tem William Lynn deciding today's motion.

Discussed, not ARGUED. The Court modifes and adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CAMILLE COLARUSSO Ntc Of Mtn Compelling The Court To Enforce The Order Dated October 24th 2023 To Respond To Discovery And Interrogatories. Motion denied without prejudice. The notice of motion is defective and is unaccompanied by a proof of service. The requirements of C.C.P. 1005 was not met by the moving party. Moving party also failed to provide a courtesy copy as required by local rules.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion Amd Motion The Court For An Order Compelling Response To Interrogatories Served On Defendant December 29, 2022
Off Calendar Oct-18-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Camille Colarusso, self-represented Plaintiff, appearing for herself and specially appearing for Vincent Yu, counsel for Defendant.

Hearing re: PLAINTIFF CAMILLE COLARUSSO'S Motion The Court For An Order Compelling Response To Interrogatories Served On Defendant December 29, 2022.

Ms. Colarusso represents that she had spoken with Mr. Yu, that Mr. Yu had a conflict today, that he asked her to make a special appearance on his behalf today, and that he informed her that he would provide the answers.

At the conclusion of Ms. Colarusso's comments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

The unopposed motion is granted. Defendant shall answer Set One Interrogatories, without objections, within 15 days.

Ms. Colarusso is to prepare a form of order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to Judge Pro Tem Cohen at: tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter Not Reported. (302/JPT)
